reviewer.evidencePolicy: Only mark evidenceStatus=satisfied for the three claim-discovery workflow claims covered by canonical-map tests, review-input tests, live packets, and explicit proof boundaries.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names deterministic packet and curation artifact fields: absorbed raw claim ids and fingerprints.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by canonical-map generator tests and live canonical-map output that preserve raw claim ids in mappings and coverage records, and preserve claimFingerprint both per mapping and per absorbed raw claim.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep semantic-review confidence separate from the raw-id and fingerprint preservation guarantee.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names a machine-readable mapping artifact and projection from raw claims to canonical user-facing or maintainer-facing claim ids.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by the checked-in .cautilus/claims/canonical-claim-map.json packet and a live regenerated packet with schemaVersion cautilus.canonical_claim_map.v1, 365 raw claims, and all raw claims projected to user or maintainer canonical ids.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep canonical-map generation connected to the maintained user and maintainer spec indexes.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names deterministic CLI packet preparation behavior and explicitly excludes LLM review and result application.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by CLI and runtime tests plus a live `claim review prepare-input` packet with schemaVersion cautilus.claim_review_input.v1, bounded clusters, skippedClusters, skippedClaims, selectionPolicy, and a packetNotice stating it does not call an LLM or assert satisfaction. The CLI handler only builds and writes review input.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep reviewer launch and review-result application as separate workflow branches from prepare-input.
